hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in General Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 13 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #22.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in General Management is the broadest specialisation, covering strategic management, financial services, SHRM, brand management, and operations planning. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at ₹2,25,000. Best for senior professionals, career switchers, and roles spanning multiple management functions.

about.body:
General Management is the most versatile MBA specialisation — chosen by professionals who want broad management capability across strategy, finance, HR, marketing, and operations rather than depth in a single domain. The Amity Online MBA in General Management covers strategic management, management of financial services, strategic human resource management, product and brand management, and operations planning and scheduling across 13 Sem 3-4 subjects.

The specialisation suits senior professionals managing cross-functional teams who need a management credential without narrowing to a specific domain, career switchers moving from technical or non-management roles into general management positions, and entrepreneurs who need a broad foundation rather than a specialist credential.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The General Management MBA carries the Amity University name on the final certificate, identical to the campus degree. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, Blue Ocean, and balanced scorecard to corporate competitive strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Management of Financial Services
sem3Subjects.description: Understand banking, insurance, capital markets, and NBFC operations for managing financial service organisations.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Human Resource Management
sem3Subjects.description: Align HR strategy with business goals through workforce planning, talent management, and culture building.

sem3Subjects.name: Product and Brand Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build product portfolios, manage brand equity, and lead product launches across consumer and B2B markets.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Planning
sem3Subjects.description: Design production planning, capacity management, and aggregate demand forecasting for operational efficiency.

sem3Subjects.name: Scheduling and Control
sem3Subjects.description: Apply project scheduling, critical path method, and resource control techniques to operational management.

sem3Subjects.name: Professional Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Examine corporate governance, business ethics frameworks, and CSR obligations for senior management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Minor Project
sem3Subjects.description: Apply Sem 3 general management concepts to a multi-function business problem with documented analysis.

sem4Subjects.name: Big Data Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Understand how large-scale data systems inform strategic and operational decisions in modern organisations.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Commerce - Reinventing Business Models
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse how digital commerce disrupts traditional business models and creates new strategic opportunities.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Apply digital marketing strategy across channels to build brand presence and drive commercial outcomes.

sem4Subjects.name: Management in Action - Social Economic and Ethical Issues
sem4Subjects.description: Examine the broader social and economic responsibilities of senior managers in organisational contexts.

sem4Subjects.name: Major Project
sem4Subjects.description: Execute a capstone management strategy project addressing a real cross-functional business challenge.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core. See the main Amity MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
General Management MBA graduates from Amity University Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, Services, Technology, BFSI, Government-adjacent organisations, Conglomerates

**Entry-level roles:** Management Trainee, Operations Executive, Business Development Executive, Project Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Business Manager, Operations Manager, Strategy Manager, General Manager (mid-size company)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Country Manager, Business Unit Head, CEO (SME), Director of Operations

**Top hiring organisations:** Tata Group management trainee programmes, Mahindra Group, Government PSU management boards, mid-size manufacturing companies

skills.body:
The General Management specialisation builds cross-functional skills tested at senior management interview and assessment stages:

**Technical skills:**
- Strategic Management: competitor analysis, strategic options evaluation, and strategy implementation planning
- Operations Planning: production planning, capacity management, and aggregate demand forecasting for operational efficiency
- Financial Services Management: understanding banking, insurance, and NBFC operations for cross-functional managers

**Applied skills:**
- Brand and Product Management: product lifecycle management, brand equity measurement, and portfolio decisions
- Digital Commerce and Big Data Literacy: understanding data-driven decisions and digital business models at strategic level

**Soft skills:** Leading cross-functional business reviews, presenting strategy to board, managing multi-department teams spanning finance, HR, and marketing.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA General Management against peers:

**vs LPU General Management MBA:** LPU is NAAC A++ and NIRF #31 at lower fees. LPU's General Management spec covers similar cross-functional breadth. Amity's advantage is NIRF #22 and QS ranking, which matter for MNC and large corporate screening. Both are solid options for working professionals needing a broad credential.

**vs Chandigarh University General Management MBA:** CU is NAAC A+ and NIRF #19, slightly better ranking than Amity. Fee is similar. CU has strong placement infrastructure for general management roles in manufacturing and services in north India. Amity has stronger pan-India brand recognition. Choose based on target employer geography.

**vs Sikkim Manipal University Online MBA:** SMU is NAAC A+. It does not appear in NIRF 2025. General Management is its primary spec. Amity's NIRF #22 ranking is a clear differentiator for employers who screen by NIRF. SMU is lower cost and may suit budget-constrained candidates, but Amity's brand is substantially stronger.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Amity University Online MBA in General Management is ₹2,25,000 across 4 semesters. Per-semester fee is ₹56,300. Amity does not charge a separate registration fee.

For working professionals, Amity offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately ₹8,906 per month through its NBFC lending partners.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amity University Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (General Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. General Management is the most versatile credential as employers in all sectors recognise it without needing to evaluate a domain-specific specialisation.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA General Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Suresh Babu
items.liked: Strategic management module gave me tools to lead strategy reviews at my company for the first time
items.disliked: Operations planning content was more manufacturing-focused, could use more service industry examples
items.body: I am a senior engineer managing cross-functional project teams. The MBA gave me the management vocabulary and frameworks I was missing to lead business reviews. The strategic management and financial services content was directly applicable to my work at an infrastructure company. Got promoted to Business Head within a year. Amity brand was unquestioned by our HR department.

items.name: Meenakshi Iyer
items.liked: Cross-functional curriculum was exactly what I needed as an operations manager covering multiple functions
items.disliked: Big data module was brief — a week spent on analytics at management level is not enough context
items.body: I was an operations manager being considered for General Manager. The MBA gave me the financial services and strategic HR content I was missing alongside my operations background. Passed the General Manager assessment at my company 6 months after completion. The Amity NIRF #22 was specifically checked and confirmed by our HR Director during the promotion process.

items.name: Amit Sharma
items.liked: SHRM and brand management combination was exactly right for a family business management role
items.disliked: Digital commerce module could be more India-specific, relied on global case studies
items.body: I manage my family's manufacturing business. The General Management spec covered everything I needed — strategy, HR management, and brand management — without forcing me into one functional silo. The Amity credential was well received when we tendered for a large government contract that required management degree proof. Good value for a family business context.

faqs.question: Is General Management a weak choice compared to a specialised MBA spec?
faqs.answer: It depends on your career goal. For domain-specific roles — Finance Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Data Scientist — a specialised spec is stronger. For General Manager, Business Head, Operations Director, or entrepreneurial roles that span multiple functions, General Management is the right choice. Senior professionals managing cross-functional teams are often better served by breadth than depth.

faqs.question: Does Amity General Management MBA include the same subjects as the Business Analytics or Finance spec?
faqs.answer: No. Each spec has distinct Sem 3-4 subjects. General Management covers strategy, financial services, strategic HR, product management, and operations planning. Business Analytics covers Power BI, supervised learning, and forecasting. Finance covers corporate finance, taxation, and valuation. Choose based on which subject areas match your role requirements and career direction.

faqs.question: Is General Management valid for government PSU recruitment notifications?
faqs.answer: Yes, provided the notification accepts UGC-DEB online degrees, which most central government notifications now do. The General Management specialisation is broad enough to qualify for most management post notifications. Always verify the specific recruitment notification's eligibility criteria before applying — some notifications specify functional specialisations.

faqs.question: What salary outcomes does General Management MBA produce?
faqs.answer: General Management outcomes depend heavily on prior experience. Freshers may see Rs 4-8 lakh in management trainee roles. Professionals with 5-10 years of experience commonly transition to Business Manager or Operations Head roles paying Rs 12-25 lakh. Senior professionals with 10+ years leveraging the degree for leadership credentialing see the most significant career impact.

faqs.question: Can I start as General Management and later do a certification to add a specialisation?
faqs.answer: Yes. The MBA General Management credential combined with domain-specific certifications (PMP for project management, CMA for finance, SHRM-CP for HR) is a common strategy for senior professionals who want both breadth and a recognised domain credential. The MBA opens the management track; certifications signal specific functional expertise.

chooseIf: You are a senior professional managing cross-functional teams who needs a management credential covering strategy, HR, finance, marketing, and operations without being locked into one domain.
You are an entrepreneur or family business manager wanting formal management education across all functions.
You are a career switcher from a technical or non-management background entering a general management or business development role.
consider: You are targeting a specific domain role — Finance, HR, Marketing, or IT — a specialised spec is more credible to domain-specific employers.
You want the broadest possible MBA brand for premium management roles — NMIMS Online or Symbiosis Online General Management carry stronger brand at large corporates.
You want cross-functional management training with a stronger analytics component — General Management at Manipal Online includes Coursera certifications with broader coverage.
